AI600_1RiskAreaOut.description: One risk area in the NIST AI 600-1 GenAI Profile.
AI600_1RiskAreaOut.title: AI600_1RiskAreaOut

ActiveSessionRow.description:
One active session.

`extra="allow"` because the underlying session dict carries variable
metadata (user_agent, ip, geo enrichment, started_at_utc, etc.) that
we don't want to drop. Explicit fields cover the read-paths in
static/analytics-usage.html.
ActiveSessionRow.title: ActiveSessionRow

AdversarialRunResponse.description:
Response shape for POST /api/security/adversarial/run.

Permissive (`extra="allow"`) because `run_adversarial_suite()` returns a
deeply nested structure that evolves per probe set. The explicit `error`
field is the only stable contract callers branch on.
AdversarialRunResponse.title: AdversarialRunResponse

AgentBindingOut.description:
AgentBinding record. Loosely typed; underlying shape from domain.agent_bindings.

list_bindings returns enriched dicts with agent_name/agent_team/version_semver
added on top of the binding fields; extra='allow' permits the enrichment.
AgentBindingOut.title: AgentBindingOut

AgentOut.description: Agent record. Loosely typed; underlying shape from domain.agents.Agent.
AgentOut.title: AgentOut

AgentSubscriberOut.description: AgentSubscriber record. Loosely typed; underlying shape from domain.agent_subscribers.
AgentSubscriberOut.title: AgentSubscriberOut

AgentVersionOut.description: AgentVersion record. Loosely typed; underlying shape from domain.agents.AgentVersion.
AgentVersionOut.title: AgentVersionOut

AiSystemContextOut.description: Compact AI system context surfaced beside the evals list.
AiSystemContextOut.title: AiSystemContextOut

AiSystemDetailOut.description:
AI system detail view: summary fields + enrichments.

Extending AiSystemSummaryOut keeps the field list authoritative in one place.
`model_config` is inherited; `extra='forbid'` still applies.

evidence.description: Evidence linked to this system (EvidenceSummaryOut shape; loosely typed for V1 compat).
evidence.title: Evidence

findings.description: Findings linked to this system (FindingDetailOut shape; loosely typed for V1 compat).
findings.title: Findings

recent_events.description: Runtime events for this system (HomepageRuntimeEventOut shape; loosely typed for V1 compat).
recent_events.title: Recent Events

release_gates.description: GateResultOut shape if results exist; null otherwise. Loosely typed for V1 compat.
release_gates.title: Release Gates

AiSystemDetailOut.title: AiSystemDetailOut

AiSystemSummaryOut.description: Compact AI system row for the portfolio list view.
AiSystemSummaryOut.title: AiSystemSummaryOut

AiSystemsListOut.description: Portfolio list response. `total` is cheap to compute (mock data).
AiSystemsListOut.title: AiSystemsListOut

AnalyticsByDomainResponse.description: Domain-scoped subset of the analytics rollup.
AnalyticsByDomainResponse.title: AnalyticsByDomainResponse

AnalyticsResponse.description:
Full analytics rollup.

Permissive: empty-history path returns 8 keys, populated path returns 10.
by_domain/by_model/failure_types are dynamic dicts keyed on real-world
names (domain, model id, metric name) so they're typed as plain dicts.
AnalyticsResponse.title: AnalyticsResponse

AnalyticsTrendsResponse.description: Time-series subset of the analytics rollup.
AnalyticsTrendsResponse.title: AnalyticsTrendsResponse

ApprovalOut.description: A human-approval request (gates a high-risk action).
ApprovalOut.title: ApprovalOut

use_case.description: UseCase enum value -- set by endpoint if omitted
use_case.title: Use Case

AskResponseOut.description:
Response from any /ask|/summarize-*|/explain-*|/draft-* endpoint.

These endpoints are LLM-triggering -- response carries GovernanceMetadata
for downstream CISO Console correlation (trace_id linkage to App Insights).
AskResponseOut.title: AskResponseOut

AssessmentReportResponse.description:
Full assessment report.

Mirrors domain.assessment_engine.AssessmentReport. Permissive at the top
level so the engine layer can add fields without breaking the SPA. Nested
models are strict — their domain shapes are stable.
AssessmentReportResponse.title: AssessmentReportResponse

AuditEventsResponse.description:
Response from GET /api/audit/events.

`events` is list[dict[str, Any]] because audit events are heterogeneous --
every event_type carries different payload fields. Tightening per event_type
is a Phase 1.5 follow-up; for now Schemathesis fuzzes the envelope only.
AuditEventsResponse.title: AuditEventsResponse

AuditRowOut.description: One row in the provider audit log.
AuditRowOut.title: AuditRowOut

AwsDemoFullResponse.description:
Full walkthrough payload.

Permissive: top-level keys each hold entirely different nested structures
(intake metadata, risk classification tables, mermaid diagrams, raw HTML,
etc.).  A closed schema would reject valid payload — extra="allow" is the
correct compound-27a choice for heterogeneous / asymmetric top-level dicts.
AwsDemoFullResponse.title: AwsDemoFullResponse

AwsDemoStepResponse.description:
Single step slice of the walkthrough payload.

``data`` is typed ``Any`` because each step key returns a completely
different structure (dict, list, str, int) — a union type would provide
false precision on a truly open-ended shape.
AwsDemoStepResponse.title: AwsDemoStepResponse

BatchHistoryRowOut.description: One row in the batch history list.
BatchHistoryRowOut.title: BatchHistoryRowOut

BatchPrompt.description: A single prompt in a batch.
BatchPrompt.title: BatchPrompt

BatchResultOut.description:
Synchronous result of /api/batch/run or /api/batch/run-domain/{id}.

Returned 200 even on partial errors -- individual runs surface their
error in the per-run dict (BatchRunResultOut). The top-level `error`
field is set ONLY when the entire batch couldn't start (e.g. no prompts).
Preserves V1 batch.html contract.

error.description: Top-level error -- batch did not run. None on success.
error.title: Error

BatchResultOut.title: BatchResultOut

BatchRunResultOut.description:
One (test_case, model) run result.

Loosely typed -- the original V1 UI introspects many sub-fields
(eval_scores per-metric, guardrails sub-dict) that vary by config.
Phase 1.5 can tighten if V2 SPA needs strict types.
BatchRunResultOut.title: BatchRunResultOut

ByUserRow.description: Per-user activity rollup. Shape is bounded (domain.usage_analytics line 302).
ByUserRow.title: ByUserRow

CascadeResultOut.description:
Full result of a Right-to-Forget cascade.

`governance` carries chain_hash + trace_id when populated (Phase 1.5).
The per-step sha256_digest_after fields already provide tamper-evident
digests of each store post-purge; chain_hash is the audit-log event hash.
CascadeResultOut.title: CascadeResultOut

CompletenessRowOut.description:
Single completeness row.

Mirrors domain.evidence_repository.CompletenessRow (dataclass).
`pct` is rounded to 1 decimal upstream; `missing` is already capped
at 10-15 items per row depending on axis.
CompletenessRowOut.title: CompletenessRowOut

ConflictDetail.description:
Structured 409 body for typed conflicts (gate denials, idempotency, state transitions).

Vocabulary locked at the 4 values below per audit doc §10 resolution #3.
Adding a new value = minor __version__ bump.
ConflictDetail.title: ConflictDetail

ConnectorResultsResponse.description:
Cumulative outputs for a single connector across all syncs.

Each list contains domain-model dumps (EvalResult / Finding /
RuntimeEvent / Evidence). Typed as list[dict] — see module docstring
for why we don't bind to the full domain Pydantic models here.
ConnectorResultsResponse.title: ConnectorResultsResponse

ConnectorStatusOut.description: Status of one runtime connector (Langfuse / AWS CloudTrail / Datadog).
ConnectorStatusOut.title: ConnectorStatusOut

ConnectorSummary.description:
Per-connector status + cumulative output counts.

Shape produced by domain.connectors.list_connectors_summary().
All 11 fields always present — strict (no extra="allow").
ConnectorSummary.title: ConnectorSummary

ControlsBreakdownOut.description: Breakdown of controls by priority + domain.
ControlsBreakdownOut.title: ControlsBreakdownOut

DemoResetResponse.description: Confirmation that the demo state was wiped.
DemoResetResponse.title: DemoResetResponse

DemoRunResponse.description:
Response from demo run.

Permissive: each run record contains nested eval_scores and guardrail
results whose key sets vary by model, domain, and guardrail pipeline
version. extra="allow" lets those dynamic payloads flow through unchanged.
DemoRunResponse.title: DemoRunResponse

DemoStateResponse.description:
Current demo walkthrough state.

Fixed 5-key shape: system identity plus step-completion tracking and
cached assessment snapshots for v1 (pre-remediation) and v2 (post).
DemoStateResponse.title: DemoStateResponse

DemoStepResponse.description:
Response envelope for a single demo step execution.

Permissive (extra="allow"): each of the 13 steps returns a different
payload shape beyond the common step/title keys — controls lists,
assessment scores, connector run arrays, evidence attachments, finding
transition records, and before/after delta dicts. A closed schema would
either reject valid step-specific keys or require every field to be
Optional, making the contract meaningless. extra="allow" is the correct
choice per the asymmetric/polymorphic exemption (compound 27a).
DemoStepResponse.title: DemoStepResponse

DomainConfig.description: Schema for creating/updating a domain (request body).
DomainConfig.title: DomainConfig

DomainListResponse.description:
Envelope for GET / (list).

`domains` is list[dict] per compound 27a — domain JSON files carry
arbitrary keys and picker consumers read few fields; decoupling
OpenAPI from per-domain schema drift is the goal here.
DomainListResponse.title: DomainListResponse

DomainOut.description:
Single-domain response: DomainConfig fields + id.

`extra="allow"` tolerates legacy keys in stored JSON files without
forcing a schema migration. Single-record consumers (domains.html
edit modal) read many fields, so a strict mirror is worthwhile.
DomainOut.title: DomainOut

EditInfoOut.description: Field tiers + rerun matrix + approval rules + current pending status.

approval_roles_by_level.description: Map of risk level (LOW/MEDIUM/.../CRITICAL) -> required approver roles.
approval_roles_by_level.title: Approval Roles By Level

field_tiers.description: Map of tier name (soft/material/critical) -> list of field names.
field_tiers.title: Field Tiers

rerun_matrix.description: Map of field name -> downstream steps that must re-run on change.
rerun_matrix.title: Rerun Matrix

EditInfoOut.title: EditInfoOut

EditStatusOut.description: Pending-edit status for one AI system.
EditStatusOut.title: EditStatusOut

EffectiveStateOut.description:
AI system with approved revisions folded in + the base + status.

`ai_system` and `base` are typed as opaque dicts because the AI system
model has 22+ fields and is re-typed in api.grc.AiSystemDetailOut;
duplicating that here would create drift. Phase 1.5 can unify these.
EffectiveStateOut.title: EffectiveStateOut

EpisodeItem.description: Single episode row returned in list responses.
EpisodeItem.title: EpisodeItem

EvalOverviewRowOut.description: Per-system summary row for the Evals page index.
EvalOverviewRowOut.title: EvalOverviewRowOut

EvalResultOut.description:
One eval result with framework/control mappings + sample failures.

Wire shape from seed.EVAL_RESULTS[*].model_dump(mode='json') + computed
pass_rate.
EvalResultOut.title: EvalResultOut

EvalScore.description:
Per-metric evaluation result.

score and passed may be None when the evaluator cannot produce a
value (e.g. missing reference). details is always a string.
EvalScore.title: EvalScore

EvalScoreEntry.description:
One metric result inside eval_scores.

Keys beyond score/passed/details are permitted via extra="allow" because
some evaluators attach supplemental fields (e.g. "details", "rationale").
EvalScoreEntry.title: EvalScoreEntry

EvaluateResponse.description:
Envelope returned by POST /evaluate.

eval_scores is keyed on dynamic metric names supplied by the
evaluator, so the envelope carries ConfigDict(extra="allow").
The inner EvalScore shape is strict.
On error eval_scores is None and error carries a short message.
EvaluateResponse.title: EvaluateResponse

EventRow.description:
One audit event (JSONL record).

`extra="allow"` because events are polymorphic by event_type — LOGIN
carries ip/city/region/country/user_agent; PAGE_VIEW carries path;
domain events carry their own payload. Explicit fields are the common
superset read by static/analytics-usage.html.
EventRow.title: EventRow

EvidenceDetailResponse.description:
Single Evidence record + resolved AI system name.

Mirrors domain.models.Evidence (Pydantic v2) field-by-field with
datetimes serialized as ISO strings (model_dump(mode="json")), plus
the resolved `ai_system_name` joined from the parent AI system. Kept
strict — the Evidence domain model is stable and audit clients
fetching by id benefit from a typed shape.
EvidenceDetailResponse.title: EvidenceDetailResponse

EvidenceRowOut.description:
Single evidence record as rendered in the sectioned roll-up.

Mirrors domain.evidence_repository.EvidenceRow (dataclass). 18
fields, all always present. Optionals reflect the source dataclass:
assessment_id / hash / uri may be None.
EvidenceRowOut.title: EvidenceRowOut

FieldChangeOut.description: One field's before/after in a revision.
FieldChangeOut.title: FieldChangeOut

FindingEventOut.description: One event from apply_event() or list_events() -- includes finding_id.
FindingEventOut.title: FindingEventOut

FindingExceptionOut.description: Exception/waiver attached to a finding. Sparse model -- tightened in Phase 1.5.
FindingExceptionOut.title: FindingExceptionOut

FindingTimelineEventOut.description: One event embedded inline in a finding's timeline (no finding_id -- implicit).
FindingTimelineEventOut.title: FindingTimelineEventOut

FindingV2Out.description:
Full finding record (workflow shape from domain.findings_workflow).

Differs from api.grc.FindingOut: this is the workflow view (has timeline,
exception, mapped controls/frameworks expanded), GRC view is the summary.
Two intentional shapes for two consumers.
FindingV2Out.title: FindingV2Out

GateDefinitionOut.description: Static catalog definition of a release gate.
GateDefinitionOut.title: GateDefinitionOut

GateEvaluationOut.description: Per-gate evaluation result for one system.
GateEvaluationOut.title: GateEvaluationOut

GateExceptionOut.description: An approved exception/waiver for a failed gate.
GateExceptionOut.title: GateExceptionOut

GateReportOut.description: Full gate report for one system (target environment).

blocking_failures.description: Count of blocking gates that failed.
blocking_failures.title: Blocking Failures

GateReportOut.title: GateReportOut

blocking_failures.description: Count of blocking gates that failed.
blocking_failures.title: Blocking Failures

GovernanceMetadata.description:
Cross-cutting governance overlay per docs/target-architecture.md §5.

Populated on responses from endpoints that trigger the decorator chain
(@policy_gate -> @scrub_pii -> @guardrails -> @trace_llm_call -> @evaluate_response).
Pure CRUD / catalog endpoints omit this field.

SPA UX hook: CISO Console "explain finding" flyout reads trace_id and links
directly to App Insights via the operation_Id correlation.

chain_hash.description: SHA-256 hash from audit_chain.py for the originating event.
chain_hash.title: Chain Hash

eval_scores.description: DeepEval 6-metric scores when evaluator ran; keys per evaluator.py.
eval_scores.title: Eval Scores

trace_id.description: Langfuse trace_id (also App Insights operation_Id; identical value).
trace_id.title: Trace Id

vault_id.description: Scrubber vault reference if PII was tokenised; None if no PII present.
vault_id.title: Vault Id

GovernanceMetadata.title: GovernanceMetadata

GuardrailCheckRequest.description: Request to check input/output via guardrails.
GuardrailCheckRequest.title: GuardrailCheckRequest

GuardrailCheckResponse.description:
Response shape for POST /api/security/guardrails/check.

Unioned input/output shape: `apply_guardrails` returns the `allowed/reason/
violation/prompt` tuple; `filter_output` returns the `safe/response/violations`
tuple. Both fields are optional so either branch validates.
GuardrailCheckResponse.title: GuardrailCheckResponse

GuardrailsSummaryResponse.description:
Response shape for GET /api/security/guardrails/summary.

Permissive because the rail summary is intentionally diagnostic and the
inner shape (input_rails / output_rails) is documented by example, not pinned.
GuardrailsSummaryResponse.title: GuardrailsSummaryResponse

HomepageFindingOut.description:
Homepage critical-finding row (portfolio aggregation shape).

NOTE: different shape from FindingOut. Portfolio aggregator returns a
pre-formatted row with `system` (name) and `time_ago` (display), not the
full finding record.

severity.description: P0 / P1 / etc. — portfolio uses priority labels, not CRITICAL/HIGH.
severity.title: Severity

HomepageFindingOut.title: HomepageFindingOut

HomepageRuntimeEventOut.description:
Homepage runtime event row (portfolio aggregation shape).

NOTE: this is a DIFFERENT shape from RuntimeEventOut below. The homepage
strip is fed by domain.portfolio.homepage_runtime_events() which joins
runtime events with policy/control/framework context; the /runtime/events
endpoint returns the raw mock_data shape (id/timestamp/system_id/...).
Two different shapes for two different consumers -- locked here so
Schemathesis catches future drift.

time.description: HH:MM display string, derived from timestamp.
time.title: Time

IncidentUpdateOut.title: IncidentUpdateOut

All fields gathered across the 5 wizard steps. Most are optional during
preview; submit performs final validation.

rationale.description: Multi-line rationale; each entry is one rule outcome explanation.

IntakeSubmitOut.description: Result of POST /api/grc/intake/submit -- the new system_id + redirect.
IntakeSubmitOut.title: IntakeSubmitOut

Intake-created systems (read from JSONL).

`systems` is list[dict] -- the AISystem domain model has 30+ fields and
is already typed in api.grc.AiSystemDetailOut. Phase 1.5 can unify.

KpisOut.description:
Top-level KPI dashboard for command center.

All fields populated by domain.portfolio.compute_kpis() against live mock data.
Adding a field here = minor __version__ bump.

sla.description: Finding SLA buckets (overdue, due_7d, due_30d, closed).
sla.title: Sla

MatrixRowOut.description: One row in the coverage matrix.
MatrixRowOut.title: MatrixRowOut

NistFunctionOut.description: One NIST AI RMF function (GOVERN / MAP / MEASURE / MANAGE).
NistFunctionOut.title: NistFunctionOut

NistRmfOut.description: NIST AI Risk Management Framework posture.
NistRmfOut.title: NistRmfOut

NotificationItemOut.description: One notification in the AI Risk Operations notification center.
NotificationItemOut.title: NotificationItemOut

NotificationResolveOut.description:
Echo of the resolved notification + ok flag.

Per audit doc §10 resolution #4: state-changing mutations echo the resource
state so the client doesn't need a re-fetch.
NotificationResolveOut.title: NotificationResolveOut

categories.description: Map of category enum -> display label (CRITICAL_FINDING -> 'Critical Findings' etc.).
categories.title: Categories

OkResponse.description:
Minimal mutation acknowledgment for fire-and-forget endpoints.

Use ONLY when the server has no resource state to echo back to the client
(e.g. notifications/reset, purge endpoints). For state-changing mutations
where the client wants the new state without a re-fetch, return the typed
resource directly (e.g. notifications/{id}/resolve -> NotificationOut).
OkResponse.title: OkResponse

OwaspItemOut.description: One OWASP Top 10 item (LLM or Agentic AI).
OwaspItemOut.title: OwaspItemOut

PolicyDecisionOut.description:
Loose typed wrapper for explain_provider_decision() output.

The underlying dispatch returns different shapes depending on which branch
fires (blocked / re-check blocked / allowed). Keep extra='allow' for now;
tighten when downstream Phase 1.5 plumbs the values cleanly.
PolicyDecisionOut.title: PolicyDecisionOut

PolicySummaryOut.description: Aggregate stats across all providers + audit log.
PolicySummaryOut.title: PolicySummaryOut

ProbeCategory.description: One adversarial probe category and its enrolled probes.
ProbeCategory.title: ProbeCategory

ProjectionViewResponse.description:
Response body for GET /api/projection/views/{view}.

``rows`` is intentionally typed ``list[dict[str, Any]]`` because the endpoint
serves five different Postgres tables (ai_systems, eval_runs, findings,
release_decisions, policy_evaluations) and each has its own column shape.
A single strict model would either become a giant union or force five
sibling endpoints — both worse than honest polymorphism. See
ARCHITECTURE.md compound rule 27a (list[dict] justified for asymmetric /
polymorphic responses).

JSONB columns are returned as JSON-encoded *strings*, not nested objects
(see api/projection.py L278-281). SDK consumers should ``json.loads`` those
columns explicitly when needed.
ProjectionViewResponse.title: ProjectionViewResponse

ProviderOut.description:
Full provider record (catalog entry).

Note: api_key_secret_ref is the SECRET REFERENCE URI, not the secret value.
masked_key_preview is the only key-related field clients ever see.
ProviderOut.title: ProviderOut

PurgeStepOut.description: Per-store result of one cascade purge step.
PurgeStepOut.title: PurgeStepOut

RejectCascadeIn.description:
Body for POST /api/right-to-forget/{cascade_id}/reject.

Reason is required — rejection without justification breaks the audit
intent of the workflow. Mirrors CascadeRequest.reason constraints
(1-1024 chars, str_strip_whitespace).
RejectCascadeIn.title: RejectCascadeIn

ReportCatalogItem.description: One entry in the report-type catalog. Stable shape — UI keys off `type`/`title`/`scope`.
ReportCatalogItem.title: ReportCatalogItem

ReportDataResponse.description:
Permissive — the report payload shape diverges across the six report
types (executive / assessment / release_gate / framework_coverage /
findings / evidence). Pin only the three discriminators that every
builder populates; surface the rest via `extra="allow"` rather than
freezing internal builder shapes on the first sweep (compound rule 25a).
ReportDataResponse.title: ReportDataResponse

ReportSystemItem.description: One AI system entry for the per-system selector dropdown.
ReportSystemItem.title: ReportSystemItem

RequiredControlOut.description: One required control in the intake preview.

frameworks.description: Map of framework name -> list of clause IDs covered by this control.
frameworks.title: Frameworks

RequiredControlOut.title: RequiredControlOut

RevisionOut.description:
One revision (edit submission) record.

Loosely typed -- revisions carry arbitrary domain-specific fields
(approval_status, fields_changed[], rerun_steps[], etc.) that vary by tier
and approval state. Phase 1.5 can tighten.

RunAcceptedResponse.description:
Response for POST /api/demo-control/run/{scenario_id} (202 Accepted).

Terminal result is available via status_url once the run completes.
RunAcceptedResponse.title: RunAcceptedResponse

RunStatusResponse.description:
Response for GET /api/demo-control/run/{run_id}/status.

Permissive (extra="allow"): the ``result`` field carries whichever scenario
handler's payload was returned. Shapes differ across all 6 scenarios —
vault_stats dict, 14-day trend list, right-to-forget cascade model_dump,
agent publish dict, PDF pack hash. A closed schema would reject valid
payloads. Same bare-by-design rationale as DemoRunResponse (S38, compound
27a).
RunStatusResponse.title: RunStatusResponse

RuntimeActionOut.description: Audit-log entry produced by every runtime state mutation.

action_type.description: ENABLE | DISABLE | KILL_SWITCH | RESET_KILL_SWITCH | SET_MONITORING etc.
action_type.title: Action Type

RuntimeActionOut.title: RuntimeActionOut

RuntimeMetaOut.description: Enum vocabulary for clients (monitoring levels, approval/incident statuses).
RuntimeMetaOut.title: RuntimeMetaOut

RuntimeStateOut.description: Per-system runtime state (enabled / kill-switch / monitoring level).
RuntimeStateOut.title: RuntimeStateOut

RuntimeStateRowOut.description: One row in the all-systems state view (denormalised with name).
RuntimeStateRowOut.title: RuntimeStateRowOut

SectionCatalogItem.description: One entry in the static section catalog.
SectionCatalogItem.title: SectionCatalogItem

SectionedSection.description: One section in the sectioned roll-up response.
SectionedSection.title: SectionedSection

SimulatedRunOut.description:
Synchronous result of re-running the simulated eval suite for one system.

NOT a JobResponse -- this endpoint completes inline; no background work.
SimulatedRunOut.title: SimulatedRunOut

StatsResponse.description: Combined memory + RAG stats from GET /api/memory/stats.
StatsResponse.title: StatsResponse

SyncResultModel.description:
Single connector sync outcome.

Mirrors domain.connectors.SyncResult (dataclass). `error` is None on
success. `sample_ids` carries up to 3 ids per produced category and
is keyed on the four fixed category names (evals/findings/
runtime_events/evidence) — typed as dict[str, list[str]].
SyncResultModel.title: SyncResultModel

SystemGateSummaryOut.description:
One-line gate summary per system for the index view.

`error` is populated when evaluate_gates raised; mutually exclusive with the
success fields. Using Optional[...] everywhere keeps OpenAPI a single
consistent shape clients can branch on.

blocking_failures.description: Count of blocking gates that failed.
blocking_failures.title: Blocking Failures

SystemGateSummaryOut.title: SystemGateSummaryOut

ThreatSeriesOut.description: 7-day threat-activity series, bucketed from real runtime events.

series.description: Map of series_name -> daily counts. Series keys are stable: prompt_injection, blocked_tool_calls, policy_violations, dlp_detections.
series.title: Series

ThreatSeriesOut.title: ThreatSeriesOut

TipOut.description: Single tip detail. Polymorphic inner shape — extra=allow per 27a sub-rule.
TipOut.title: TipOut

TipsRegistryOut.description:
Tip registry envelope.

`tips` is a registry dict keyed by tip_id. Inner shape varies per tip
type (text vs. action vs. linked-control) and is consumed as a map by
static/shared.js (line 698), so the inner value is left as `dict[str, Any]`
rather than a strict model. Outer envelope stays strict.
TipsRegistryOut.title: TipsRegistryOut

TotalsOut.description: Aggregate counters in the summary window.
TotalsOut.title: TotalsOut

TraceItemOut.description:
Single trace record as returned in the recent-traces list.

eval_scores is typed as a dict keyed on metric name (e.g. "coherence")
because the set of active metrics is runtime-configured and not fixed at
schema definition time — per compound 27a asymmetric/polymorphic carve-out.
When a trace has not been evaluated the field is None.
TraceItemOut.title: TraceItemOut

_ScenarioEntry.description: A single scenario summary returned by the list endpoint.
_ScenarioEntry.title: _ScenarioEntry

status.description: PASS | FAIL | NO_EVIDENCE | PARTIAL | NOT_APPLICABLE | NOT_EVALUATED
status.title: Status

api__framework__ItemCoverageOut.description:
Full coverage payload for one framework item.

Mirrors domain.framework_coverage.ItemCoverage. Consumed by
static/governance.html and static/security.html — every field below is
read by at least one consumer (see Session 40 sweep notes).
api__framework__ItemCoverageOut.title: ItemCoverageOut

api__grc__EvidenceOut.description: An evidence artifact attached to systems/findings/controls.
api__grc__EvidenceOut.title: EvidenceOut

api__runtime_v2__RuntimeEventOut.description:
One runtime event (Langfuse / AWS / Datadog / etc.).

Distinct from api.grc.RuntimeEventOut and api.grc.HomepageRuntimeEventOut
-- three shapes named "runtime event" for three consumers.
api__runtime_v2__RuntimeEventOut.title: RuntimeEventOut

info.description: Governance substrate for enterprise AI. Six-layer architecture (see docs/target-architecture.md). Consumed by Team Workspace SPA, CISO Console SPA, signallayer Python SDK, and `sl` CLI.
info.title: AI Assurance Platform

get.description: Serve the Agent Library publish/subscribe UI.
Return the available adversarial probe categories and total probe count.

post.description:
Run the adversarial suite and stream per-probe results via SSE.

Response type: text/event-stream. Events fired (each with `event:` header):
  start  — once, with model + total_probes + categories
  probe  — once per probe, with index/total/resisted/severity/latency_ms
  done   — once at the end, with the full summary dict
  error  — only on engine failure, before the stream terminates

Empty `categories` is rejected at the boundary (422) so the SPA never
streams an empty run.

get.description:
Return a list of agents, optionally filtered by team and/or owner_type.

Returns serialised Agent dicts. Delegates to domain.agents.list_agents().

post.description:
Create a new agent in the registry.

Returns 201 + the created Agent object.
Delegates to domain.agents.create_agent().

get.description:
Return a single agent with its version history and subscriber list.

Returns 404 if the agent does not exist.
Calls domain.agents.get_agent() + list_versions() + domain.agent_subscribers.list_subscribers()
concurrently.

Open a Server-Sent Events stream for real-time agent version notifications.

LISTENs on Postgres channel `agent_update_{agent_id}`.
Yields `event: agent_update` when a NOTIFY is received from the domain layer.
Yields `: keepalive` every 25s to prevent proxy timeout.

Client disconnect cancels the stream and closes the Postgres connection.

post.description:
Create and publish a new version for the given agent.

Returns 201 + the new AgentVersion.
Triggers subscriber notifications via domain.agent_subscribers.notify_subscribers_on_publish().
Returns 404 if the agent does not exist.
Returns 400 if the semver is already taken or invalid.

get.description:
Return the list of AgentSubscribers with their binding state.

Returns 404 if the agent does not exist.
Delegates to domain.agent_subscribers.list_subscribers().

post.description:
Approve / reject / override a revision.

On state-transition or policy rejection returns 409 with ConflictDetail
carrying the structured reason (required by CISO Console for audit-artifact
rendering per audit doc §1.2).

409.description: Decision rejected by state transition or policy.

Returns application/pdf with the generated PDF bytes.

Finding #2: Content-Disposition filename is sanitised to strip non-safe chars.

Each evidence item includes `evidence_hash` (SHA-256 computed from content).
Returns 404 for unknown framework_slug or system_id.

Finding #13: item_coverage and evidence calls are now gathered concurrently
via asyncio.gather instead of sequential awaits in the loop.

post.description: Run every connector once. Useful for a single demo button.

get.description: Per-system summary for the Evals page index -- counts by status.

post.description:
Simulate re-running the eval suite for a system (synchronous).

Refreshes the run_at timestamp on each EvalResult, perturbs scores within a
small band around the current value (so re-runs feel realistic but
deterministic outcomes are preserved), then re-runs the assessment and
release gate engines and returns the fresh decisions inline.

post.description:
Live classification: returns inherent risk + required-control set
without persisting anything. Called by the wizard on every change.

Persist the AI System, create initial assessment + release gate checks.

Pipeline:
  1. Classify inherent risk
  2. Materialize AISystem
  3. Resolve required controls (P0/P1)
  4. Create initial PRE_RELEASE Assessment
  5. Generate one ReleaseGate per required control
  6. Persist all three to JSONL
  7. Return the new system id + redirect target

Return API health status plus the deployed build SHA.

Returns {"status": "ready" | "incomplete", "sha": "<40-char-or-unknown>"}.
The sha field (Session 19) lets deploy verification confirm which commit
is live without authenticating. The previous api_keys disclosure was
removed in Session 10.

schema.title: Response Health Check Api Health Get

get.description:
Assemble multi-tier context for a workload.

Delegates to domain.agent_memory.build_context() which handles T1–T4 tiers.
Returns the assembled context string ready for LLM injection.

List recent episodes for a workload.

Calls domain.agent_memory.build_context() internally — the domain layer
handles filtering and ordering.

post.description:
Write a new episodic memory entry.

Validates workload_id and outcome at the boundary via the Pydantic model.
Delegates persistence to domain.agent_memory.write_episode().

get.description:
Semantic search over episodic memory for a workload.

Delegates to domain.agent_memory.selective_recall().

Return combined memory and RAG statistics.

Calls domain.agent_memory.memory_stats() and domain.rag_engine.rag_stats()
concurrently and merges the results.

Return Prometheus exposition format metrics.

Gate 1: ``METRICS_ENABLED=true`` must be set.
Gate 2: ``X-Metrics-Token`` header must match the cached ``_METRICS_TOKEN``
        value (constant-time compare, read once at module load).

Returns:
    - ``404`` when metrics are disabled.
    - ``401`` when the token is absent or wrong.
    - ``503`` when ``prometheus_client`` is not installed.
    - ``200`` with ``text/plain; version=0.0.4`` body when all gates pass.

post.description:
Replay events from events.jsonl into the Postgres projection tables.

Privileged endpoint: requires demo-ciso, demo-risk, or demo-engineer role.
Runs synchronously in v1 (Day 10 makes it async with a job queue).

Args:
    body:    ReplayRequest with optional from_event_id.
    request: FastAPI request (used to check role).

Returns:
    JSON with events_processed count.

get.description:
Return projection lag and tailer checkpoint metadata.

Never includes event payloads in the response.

Returns:
    ProjectionStatusResponse with lag_events and lag_seconds.

Return a paginated slice of a projection materialized table.

The *view* path parameter is whitelisted against PROJECTION_VIEWS to
prevent SQL injection.  Anything not in the whitelist → 400.

Args:
    view:      Table name — must be one of PROJECTION_VIEWS.
    page:      1-based page number.
    page_size: Rows per page (max 500).

Returns:
    JSON with ``rows``, ``page``, ``page_size``, and ``total``.

post.description:
Index a document with optional PII scrubbing.

The engine returns False (not an exception) for two distinct cases:
  1. RAG disabled — no Azure creds.
  2. PII confidence > 0.7 — document rejected and logged.
We can't distinguish them from the engine's return value alone, so the
response uses indexed=False with a generic 'rejected_or_disabled' reason.
Engineers can correlate with data/rag_rejections.jsonl or stats.rag_enabled
if they need specifics — both surfaces already exist.

delete.description:
Remove a single document from the Azure AI Search index.

Returns deleted=False when RAG is disabled — same fail-soft contract as
the engine. 404 is NOT used: Azure Search delete is idempotent and the
engine has no read-before-delete, so we can't distinguish 'didn't exist'
from 'deleted successfully' without an extra round trip we don't want.

post.description:
Hybrid (BM25 + semantic vector) search over the RAG corpus.

When RAG is disabled the engine returns an empty list — we surface that
as results=[] / total=0 (200 OK), not as an error.

get.description:
Return statistics about the RAG corpus.

When RAG is disabled (no Azure creds or RAG_ENABLED=false), returns zeroed
counters with rag_enabled=false — never raises. This is the same fail-soft
contract domain.rag_engine.rag_stats() honors.
Generate compliance audit report HTML (printable to PDF).
List the six report types with metadata for the UI.
List AI systems for the per-system selector.

Print-ready HTML view. Browser print → Save as PDF.
A native PDF generator is not wired in this build; this matches the
existing /api/report/compliance pattern.

List all cascade results ordered oldest-first.

Wrapped in a CascadeListOut envelope (per audit §1.1: no bare-list responses).
The legacy V1 contract returned a bare list; SPAs / SDK target the envelope.

post.description: Initiate a Right-to-Forget cascade for a subject.

get.description: Fetch a single cascade result by cascade_id.

post.description: Approval stub for the RTF workflow. Auto-approved in MVP.

Reject an RTF cascade.

Audit-trail-only in MVP: the cascade itself runs synchronously inline
at `initiate_cascade` time (Session 08 §7.1), so by the time approve/
reject endpoints are callable, store purges have already happened.
This endpoint records an RTF_REJECTED event (with operator-supplied
reason) onto the tamper-evident audit chain. Workflow-level "block
pending approval" is Phase 1.5 work.

delete.description:
Remove a binding and unsubscribe the system from agent notifications.

Returns 204 on success.
Returns 404 if binding does not exist.

patch.description:
Update a binding's version, pinned flag, or accept a pending upgrade.

Returns 404 if binding does not exist or belongs to a different system.
Delegates to domain.agent_bindings.update_binding_version() or accept_upgrade().
